posts.text:
Americans are extremely worried about their jobs:

The perceived probability for households to find a new role within 3 months after a job loss is down to 44.0%, the 2nd-lowest on record.

This percentage has declined -12 points since October 2024.

To put this into context, the 2020 pandemic low was 46.2% while the 2013 bottom was 45.7%.

This comes as hiring activity remains at historically depressed levels.

The US hiring rate stands at 3.3%, the 2nd-lowest since the 2020 low and in-line with levels seen in the middle of the 2008 Financial Crisis.

Furthermore, employers announced plans to hire 18,061 people in January and February, down -56% from the same period in 2025.

US job market weakness is accelerating.
posts.author: KobeissiLetter

posts.text:
Warren Buffett doesn’t just want a moat around his businesses.

He wants crocodiles. Sharks. Piranhas. Gators.

Quotes from Buffett & Munger that explain why the moat is everything:

 “What I love, of course, is a big castle and a big moat with piranhas and crocodiles.”

“A truly great business must have an enduring ‘moat’ that protects excellent returns on invested capital.”

 “The key to investing is not assessing how much an industry is going to affect society… but determining the competitive advantage of any given company and, above all, the durability of that advantage.”

“The products or services that have wide, sustainable moats around them are the ones that deliver rewards to investors.”

His message to every manager he’s ever hired:

“Widen the moat. Throw crocodiles, sharks, and gators into it. Service. Quality. Cost. Patents. Location. Whatever it takes.”

Charlie Munger said it plainly:

“A moat is an intrinsic characteristic that gives the business a durable competitive advantage.”

For me, the best companies to invest in are the ones you can tell are busy building a moat.  

Companies on the forefront of new technologies that understand them better than anyone else.  

These businesses will quietly behind the scenes engineer new patents that will yield incredible ROI.

The patents the $hg $hgraf has and are developing are akin to engineering convex options on the future of tomorrow’s leading product innovations. 

It’s incredibly difficult to value them.  Still, attempting to value them leads to a definite conclusion that you must own a piece of them.
posts.author: BambroughKevin
